ocr_text:
Composing the Work an Ill-Fated Poet Never Began
Article on Russian poet Marina Tsvetayeva, whose work Tzvetan Todorov, Bulgarian-born French philosopher and literary critic, is trying to introduce to larger audience; Todorov has written Vivre Dans le Feu: Confessions (Living in Fire: Confessions), in which he has organized extracts from nine volumes of Tsvetayeva's letters, notes and diaries into what he calls autobiogaphy she never wrote; photos (M)

For These Bonds, Security Requires Some Volatility
Market Place column on debate among investors over whether convertible bonds--which can be converted into company stock--represent good buying opportunity now, or whether convertibles remain overpriced (M)

Yankees Fall to Dreaded Devil Rays
As much as the Yankees would like to see the bigger picture, as optimistic as they might be about their playoff chances with three weeks left in the season, the $200 million team from the Bronx might have to cite a last-place team from Florida as one reason their season did not go according to plan.     With a 4-3 victory at Yankee Stadium last night, the Devil Rays, comfortably in last place in the American League East, claimed their 10th victory in 14 games against the Yankees, who now trail the first-place Red Sox by four games.
